transcript.text: Steve, good morning.
This meeting is being recorded.
How's it going?
Pretty good.
Every day is a marathon, but you've to take it one day at a time.
I'm sure you can relate.
Amen.
Yes, absolutely.
There you go.
Awesome.
I know we don't have a lot of time, so we can jump right in.
And thank you for putting so much time and energy into the SoW and just being very thorough about the partnership.
I had a chance to connect with Marcel.
It didn't happen until late last night.
But I think the biggest thing we have to talk through first is just better understanding your concern around, right?
We get 12 months is a long commitment, but we really try to give you as much context and clarity around what you'd be getting from the partnership during the seven weeks of the age.
Eight-week strategy sprint where you could then opt out of the growth execution.
So the first thing Marcel just wanted me to better understand is, I guess, why would that seven weeks, like, not give you enough time to make an educated decision about, right, the path forward?
If we could talk a little bit there, I think we can get through the rest pretty quick.

Yeah, I mean, I feel like I've been transparent from the first email that I sent you about kind of the only hesitation that we have here is, is around the financial aspect and kind of balancing risk there.
And, you know, this is just consistent with that theme.
And so hopefully I got the impression from the number of people that got additive thread that I might have frustrated you all a little bit.
And that wasn't the intent.
But the, you know, the two phases are different.
One, like, phase one is strategy, phase two is execution.
And I realize there might be a little execution in phase one.
But, you know, I hope we leave phase one.
And I'm sure we'll leave phase one feeling great about things.
And which is why I'm comfortable with the default opt-in continue.
The biggest thing that I need to protect against is if things go sideways four months into phase two, what levers do I have to hold over you?
You know, much like the, and this is kind of like a little micro example where, you know, we're collaborating on something, we're collaborating on an SoW.
And I offered an adjustment and the response has just kind of been like, take it or leave it.

And so if we're four months into phase two and I'm like, hey guys, you know, maybe content isn't meeting expectations or we're past the honeymoon phase or like growthx focuses on some larger clients or something and we feel neglected as a little tiny client, what do we have to hold over you?
We need something to keep you accountable.
And if we're four months into it and you're in a position where you can just say, take it or leave it, and we have an obligation to keep paying, we have no recourse, I just can't be in that position.
That's just a, that's a complete non-starter.
I mean, so, so the, the intent is that we have, you know, in, in 99% of these universes, we just 99, five nines of, you know, 99.999% of these universes, everything is happy.
Like this is the happy path is we're just in this, you know, through the end.
And, you know, when we raise our series A early next year, we like double, triple R spend.
Cause like, I'm assuming this is going to work.
And like, there's this beautiful future where we work together, but like the, the point of doing an SoW up front is to help both parties protect against the downside.
And so we're talking about downside or unhappy paths.
There is, however unlikely it is, there is a path where things go sideways at some point in the agreement.
And the, and the thought that we have no recourse the day we kick it off, that's just crazy.
And like, I've, I, I never actually see, I mean, I've, done quite a few SoWs between my, my time at Brexit.
And, and, and, handful of logic.
And America.
We've seen such a one-sided, S.O.W.
And so, like, I've been a little surprised by the pushback because it's incredibly, it literally shifts all liability to us.
Yeah, no, thank you for, like, elaborating and just helping me better understand that.
And, I mean, one thing, you know, I've been thinking about, too, is, like, maybe, you know, the timing that would be best for the partnership is after the next raise for you guys, right?
Like, maybe things would be easier from, like, a commitment perspective at that point because on our side, like, think of it, think about it like a software contract, right?
Like, all software contracts are your commitments.

And this is, right, an agency partnership, but it's an agency partnership that encompasses every part of content strategy, creation, publishing, and we intend to, like, deliver the services in a way that's scalable, like software.
So, I think the challenge on this side is that, like, Marcel does not want to sign agreements that are not the 12-month commitment.
Because that's what we have for most of the clients.
And that's the situation we're in with having to be picky about who we work with.
And so we want to work with Logic.
We know you guys are super early stage.
And we just think, for you guys, this is an investment that is not like every month we evaluate how we're doing.
Like, this is going to take time to build you guys from a place of very little traffic to, right, the traffic that you want to see.
And so I think there's hesitation on Marcel's side around, you know, is Steve going to be ready to pull the plug on month three when it's going to take time to get this in motion?
Or, you know, how is Steve really feeling?
Like, of course, we're doing this because, like, we want to be in this long term.
But every SoW has some recourse if things go sideways.
It's just a completely standard, an SoW without any termination.
Clause of any sort is like, is wild.
You know, obviously the default course of action is to go through this.
This isn't a clause that I ever actually expect to use, but we need something.

Well, you tell me, you tell me, if we're four months into this, six months into this, and something's not working out, what, other than a promise that you guys will always make it right, what would we have, what leverage would we have to incentivize you to make it right?
I think, totally fair question, right?
And, like, it's our, like, at stake at the end of the day, right?
Like, if you have a bad experience with growthx, like, you tell everybody in your network that growthx is, like, you know, dropped the ball on a partnership that we desperately needed.
And, I mean, that's, right, to your point, like, not going to get your money back, but we have every incentive to, like, make...
Every customer we choose to partner with successful and we deploy a ton of resources.
So like if we're not making you successful, like we just wasted our time and money on a client that we could have had three other options for, right?

Like, so that's the challenge is like, you know, I'm not in a position where, you know, I can go to Marcel and say like, hey, we need to do something that is not standard on the agreements that you've signed with other clients for Logic.
when we could be working with like these two, these two clients who will commit to that instead.
And so that's, that's, that's why I was thinking, you know, maybe it's like a timing thing.
Maybe after series A is like a better timing for you guys.
Tyler, there's no universe where I would ever sign an SOW.
Like if I, if I sign an SOW like this at Brex, Pedro would, pardon my French, rip my balls off.
Like it's a ridiculous SOW.
It doesn't, it's, it's not a money thing.
It's, it's, it's just a, it's just a fairness thing.
I.
Any client who has signed that SoW, it's insane to me.
It's just an entirely unbalanced SoW.
so, if this is truly immovable, which I think is insane, because the great thing about being at small startups is that nothing is immovable.
We choose the rules, and we're flexible with the rules.
And, you know, we're taking a leap of faith with you, and there's to be a little mutual trust.
Like, a good agreement is when both parties feel like they're coming out of it, feeling good.
And, like, literally, this SoW shifts all the risk onto us.
All of it.
All I'm asking is for one clause that gives us something, if it somehow goes sideways.
If you do get larger clients that suddenly...
So, you know, you de-emphasize the work for us.
I'm not claiming, like, I'm assuming none of this will happen, but the reason these clauses exist is to protect against those things from happening.

So, like, if this is really immovable and a non-starter, then, yeah, this just isn't going to be a good arrangement, and I don't think it makes sense for either side, but I think that's an absurd stance to hold.

I've never, you know, and so that is a firm stance, and yeah, I think we've come to a conclusion, but, like, I would love to talk to Marcel about that, because, like, CEO to CEO, that's an absurd position to take.

Yeah, I definitely, I mean, appreciate that, you know, I can just share what you're saying directly with Marcel, but I think, like, from our perspective, we're giving you seven out of the eight weeks with publishing to understand if this is a partnership that you want to continue with.
We're only having you commit for, like, a year.
Thus, which would be standard for any software subscription, and there wouldn't be an ability to opt out during that year.
So we're not trying to make this ridiculous SoW.
And I think what we would be willing to do to make life easier here is that the commitment would be for six months instead of 12 months.
But when we've done eight weeks of strategy for you guys, and you've got those seven weeks to make a decision on, do I feel confident in what GrowthX can do?

When you see, like, who is working at GrowthX and delivering the content and what the expertise is and the clients we've made successful and the traffic growth that you've seen for those clients, like, we're not putting you in a position where you're taking this massive, tremendous leap of faith.

And, like, we are being upfront with you as well about, like, we don't typically work with clients that are at the stage you're at, but we want to because of what we see you guys being able to do and what we see as the opportunity for a super healthy long-term partnership.
But it's not that we're trying to create this ridiculous SOW.
It's more so like...
That is the SoW that has been signed across our clients, and we're not trying to make you feel like we're going to put you in a situation where you're not getting what you paid for, right?
Like, building this internally, hiring for this internally, going out and training an agency that will only do one piece of this puzzle.
Like, that's the reason it is what it is.
But I hear where you're coming from.
I just want to put it all out there for you.
Yeah, so I'll just say the other frustrating part of this has been, I've proposed a few things a few times.
This is the first time that anybody has proposed anything other than take it or leave it.
So thank you for finally proposing something, some kind of compromise.
The six months, yeah, yeah, I mean, I would do that.
I would sign that today.
I would say the other common alternative is, like, I proposed a termination of convenience with no penalty.
The other
Another common balancing factor on your side here would have been the discounter with some penalty.
It's a 30-day penalty, 60-day penalty.
There's ways to incentivize us to never execute that clause.
There's a lot of just feedback on the SoW.
It's mind-blowing that you've had clients sign that SoW unaltered, but that's neither here nor there.
Yeah.
And it's honestly true.

This is definitely an exception to the norm, but I understand why you're in a position where you have to be 100% confident because this is a big spend for the business prior to raising the next round.
So I completely understand your perspective, but I don't want you to think that we're playing hardball because of the stage that the company's in.

Like, this is honestly what has been signed, and, like, the six months is something that I had to, like, really tell Marcel, like, look, Logic wants to work with us, and, like, we just need to do something for them.
And, you know, he was like, all right, six months is all.
let's go.
That we're going to be able to do.
And if they're willing to sign that, then great.
Yeah, we can do that.
Because 9K2 is like the lowest of the pricing as well.
You know, like we're also getting you guys in at the cheapest you could possibly get growthx for.
So I think that's the other reason that it's been firm or felt firm is that piece, you know, but.
All right, let's do it.
I'm in.
And I know we're going to have a beautiful, long work in relation together, Tyler.
So.
I agree.
I agree.
Like all this work on both sides is going to go to a great place.
And like, I didn't want us to get so close and like not be able to find something that is mutually beneficial.
You plant a seed.
You need to make sure you get the topsoil right.
And you take care of it.
So it grows into this beautiful plant.
We're going to do great things together.
Let's just  do it.
And I.
I.
I.

This initial interaction might have given you the impression that I'm going to be a pain-in-the- client, and I've never gotten feedback that I'm a pain-in-the- client, so I promise you I won't be a-in-the- client.
It's just important to me to get these details right up front.
Honestly, the six months is still – if two people have not recommended you guys, like this is the only situation I would have done it.
Two people recommended it and six months with no recourse.
It's still quite aggressive, but as you mentioned earlier, you guys do have a great reputation.
So it's still quite a bit one-sided – it's still an imbalanced SOW, but  it, let's go.
Sounds great, Steve.

Our lawyer is, like, finishing the review of the comments that you made just because it was something we wanted the third-party expert to have the final look at, but I'll definitely make sure the team knows, like, you have been flexible with us, so, like, we should be flexible with the comments that you have.
And – .

The sections outside of the business terms, but that should be finalized by, like, tomorrow at the latest, and then we'll be able to just confirm the language with you, and we can go ahead and lock in the 8th as well, like we talked about.
Okay.
All right.
All right.
Any other, like, final questions, comments?
I'll pass this to Marcel, I promise.
All right.
Thank you.
Awesome.
See you, Bye.

default_summary.markdown_formatted:
## Meeting Purpose

[Negotiate and finalize the terms of a partnership agreement between Logic and growthx.ai.](https://fathom.video/share/CQQWDKUM1huUAE96JYusbdV4yHc5jPmn?tab=summary&timestamp=0.0)

## Key Takeaways

  - [Agreement reached on a 6-month commitment instead of 12 months, addressing Steve's concerns about risk and flexibility](https://fathom.video/share/CQQWDKUM1huUAE96JYusbdV4yHc5jPmn?tab=summary&timestamp=250.0)
  - [GrowthX maintains their standard SOW structure but compromises on contract length](https://fathom.video/share/CQQWDKUM1huUAE96JYusbdV4yHc5jPmn?tab=summary&timestamp=250.0)
  - [$9K/month pricing confirmed as the lowest tier for GrowthX services](https://fathom.video/share/CQQWDKUM1huUAE96JYusbdV4yHc5jPmn?tab=summary&timestamp=789.0)
  - [Both parties express optimism about long-term partnership potential](https://fathom.video/share/CQQWDKUM1huUAE96JYusbdV4yHc5jPmn?tab=summary&timestamp=811.0)

## Topics

### Contract Terms Negotiation

  - [Steve expressed strong concerns about the one-sided nature of the initial 12-month SOW with no termination clause](https://fathom.video/share/CQQWDKUM1huUAE96JYusbdV4yHc5jPmn?tab=summary&timestamp=81.0)
  - [Tyler explained GrowthX's standard practice of 12-month commitments, likening it to software contracts](https://fathom.video/share/CQQWDKUM1huUAE96JYusbdV4yHc5jPmn?tab=summary&timestamp=265.0)
  - [GrowthX offered a compromise of a 6-month commitment instead of 12 months](https://fathom.video/share/CQQWDKUM1huUAE96JYusbdV4yHc5jPmn?tab=summary&timestamp=609.0)
  - [Steve accepted the 6-month term, though still considering it "quite aggressive"](https://fathom.video/share/CQQWDKUM1huUAE96JYusbdV4yHc5jPmn?tab=summary&timestamp=703.0)

### Risk and Accountability

  - [Steve emphasized the need for "levers" to hold GrowthX accountable if issues arise during execution](https://fathom.video/share/CQQWDKUM1huUAE96JYusbdV4yHc5jPmn?tab=summary&timestamp=376.0)
  - [Tyler highlighted GrowthX's reputation and incentives to make partnerships successful](https://fathom.video/share/CQQWDKUM1huUAE96JYusbdV4yHc5jPmn?tab=summary&timestamp=400.0)
  - [Discussion of potential risks: neglect due to larger clients, content not meeting expectations](https://fathom.video/share/CQQWDKUM1huUAE96JYusbdV4yHc5jPmn?tab=summary&timestamp=536.0)

### Partnership Structure

  - [8-week strategy sprint, with 7 weeks of publishing included](https://fathom.video/share/CQQWDKUM1huUAE96JYusbdV4yHc5jPmn?tab=summary&timestamp=60.0)
  - [Following strategy phase, transition to growth execution phase](https://fathom.video/share/CQQWDKUM1huUAE96JYusbdV4yHc5jPmn?tab=summary&timestamp=60.0)
  - [Total partnership valued at $9K/month, described as GrowthX's lowest tier pricing](https://fathom.video/share/CQQWDKUM1huUAE96JYusbdV4yHc5jPmn?tab=summary&timestamp=789.0)

### Trust and Relationship Building

  - [Both parties expressed desire for a long-term, successful partnership](https://fathom.video/share/CQQWDKUM1huUAE96JYusbdV4yHc5jPmn?tab=summary&timestamp=811.0)
  - [Steve assured he won't be a "pain-in-the- client" despite initial tough negotiations](https://fathom.video/share/CQQWDKUM1huUAE96JYusbdV4yHc5jPmn?tab=summary&timestamp=840.0)
  - [Tyler emphasized GrowthX's selectivity in clients and belief in Logic's potential](https://fathom.video/share/CQQWDKUM1huUAE96JYusbdV4yHc5jPmn?tab=summary&timestamp=641.0)

### Legal and Administrative Next Steps

  - [GrowthX's lawyer reviewing Steve's comments on the SOW](https://fathom.video/share/CQQWDKUM1huUAE96JYusbdV4yHc5jPmn?tab=summary&timestamp=880.0)
  - [Final language to be confirmed with Steve](https://fathom.video/share/CQQWDKUM1huUAE96JYusbdV4yHc5jPmn?tab=summary&timestamp=880.0)
  - [Tentative start date of the 8th to be locked in](https://fathom.video/share/CQQWDKUM1huUAE96JYusbdV4yHc5jPmn?tab=summary&timestamp=900.0)

## Next Steps

  - [GrowthX to finalize SOW review with their lawyer by tomorrow](https://fathom.video/share/CQQWDKUM1huUAE96JYusbdV4yHc5jPmn?tab=summary&timestamp=880.0)
  - [Confirm updated SOW language with Steve](https://fathom.video/share/CQQWDKUM1huUAE96JYusbdV4yHc5jPmn?tab=summary&timestamp=880.0)
  - [Lock in partnership start date (tentatively the 8th)](https://fathom.video/share/CQQWDKUM1huUAE96JYusbdV4yHc5jPmn?tab=summary&timestamp=900.0)
  - [Tyler to relay conversation details to Marcel (GrowthX CEO)](https://fathom.video/share/CQQWDKUM1huUAE96JYusbdV4yHc5jPmn?tab=summary&timestamp=880.0)
  - [Proceed with signing the 6-month agreement](https://fathom.video/share/CQQWDKUM1huUAE96JYusbdV4yHc5jPmn?tab=summary&timestamp=787.0)

action_items.description: Ensure legal team finalizes review of Steve's SOW comments
Confirm final SOW language w/ Steve post-legal review
Lock in Aug 8 start date for Logic engagement
Relay Steve's feedback/concerns to Marcel re SOW terms
